articles.title: OPENAI'S SHIFT FROM MICROSOFT TO SOFTBANK
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to source most of its computing power from SoftBank's Stargate project by 2030, marking a significant departure from its reliance on Microsoft.

articles.title: META'S DINOV2 FOR CANCER RESEARCH
articles.summary: Orakl Oncology uses Meta's DINOv2 model to accelerate cancer drug discovery, improving efficiency by quickly analyzing organoid images to predict patient treatment responses.

articles.title: DEEPSEEK TO OPEN SOURCE PARTS OF ONLINE SERVICES CODE
articles.summary: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek plans to open source portions of its online services' code as part of an “open source week” event.

articles.title: SIGLIP2
articles.summary: SigLIP was an immensely popular joint image and text encoder model. It has now been improved in a number of axes. Most notable is the substantially improved zero-shot classification performance, which was the hallmark result of the original CLIP work.

articles.title: STEP-LEVEL CALIBRATION FOR LLM AGENTS
articles.summary: STeCa is a novel framework designed to improve LLM agents in long-horizon tasks by automatically identifying and correcting suboptimal actions.

articles.title: GEMMAX2 TRANSLATION MODEL (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: With modern post-training techniques, this 2B model trained on Gemma achieves state-of-the-art translation performance between 28 different languages.

articles.title: MOONLIGHT 16B MUON TRAINED MODEL
articles.summary: This is the first (public) large scale model trained with the Muon optimizer. It was trained for 5.7T tokens and is a very similar architecture to DeepSeek v3.

articles.title: TRITON IMPLEMENTATION OF NAIVE SPARSE ATTENTION
articles.summary: The DeepSeek NSA paper made waves last week for its scalable and efficient long context attention algorithm. However, there was no code available. This work is a Triton replication that can be slotted into any PyTorch codebase.

articles.title: LLM DEPLOYMENT WITH OMNISERVE
articles.summary: OmniServe offers a unified framework for efficient large-scale LLM deployment, combining innovations in low-bit quantization and sparse attention to enhance both speed and cost-effectiveness.

articles.title: CUDA FOR PYTHON PROGRAMMERS
articles.summary: A great introduction to CUDA programming for those familiar with Python programming.

articles.title: US AI SAFETY INSTITUTE BUDGET CUTS
articles.summary: This article explores the potential impacts of funding cuts to the US AI Safety Institute, including implications for national security, AI research, and international competition.

articles.title: MICROSOFT PREPARES FOR OPENAI'S GPT-5 MODEL
articles.summary: Microsoft is preparing to host OpenAI's GPT-4.5 model as early as next week, with a more significant GPT-5 release anticipated by late May. The GPT-5 system will integrate OpenAI's new o3 reasoning model, aiming to create a unified AI functionality. Both releases align with key tech events, such as Microsoft Build and Google I/O, underscoring Microsoft's strategic positioning in the AI space.

articles.title: PARALLELIZING MUON
articles.summary: Various novel strategies to parallelize the up-and-coming Muon optimizer.

articles.title: CHATGPT REACHES 400M WEEKLY ACTIVE USERS
articles.summary: ChatGPT has reached 400 million weekly active users, doubling its count since August 2024.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S AI CO-SCIENTIST IS 'TEST-TIME SCALING' ON STEROIDS. WHAT THAT MEANS FOR RESEARCH
articles.summary: Google has enhanced its Gemini 2.0 LLM to generate scientific hypotheses significantly faster than human researchers.
